site.title: Athena's Psychology Pages
site.subtitle: A coquette blue notebook for reflective psychology writing, study notes, and research-minded posts.
site.heroTitle: Soft study notes, clinical curiosity, and evidence-based reflections.
site.heroIntro: This blog is part diary, part academic portfolio. I use it to turn psychology concepts into clear, thoughtful posts that feel personal without losing rigour.
site.pinnedTitle: Why I care about accessible psychological writing
site.pinnedExcerpt: A pinned note on translating research into warm, readable language for students, future clients, and wider audiences.
site.currentlyReadingTitle: The Body Keeps the Score + lecture notes on cognitive bias
site.currentlyReadingBody: I am especially interested in how trauma, memory, and meaning-making shape behaviour and therapeutic outcomes.
site.newsletterText: A gentle monthly round-up of psychology notes, reflections from King's, and useful resources I would actually recommend.
site.aboutTitle: Athena | Year 2 Psychology at King's College London
site.aboutBody: I am Athena, a second-year psychology student at King's College London with a growing interest in clinical psychology, reflective writing, and research communication. I want this space to show that academic work can be elegant, readable, and genuinely helpful. My long-term direction is entrepreneurial clinical psychology: combining strong evidence, warm communication, and creative public-facing work.
aboutHighlights: Interested in student wellbeing and mental health literacy
